get.summary: List audit log entries. Admin only.
get.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] when the caller is not an admin, or
[`ApiError::Internal`] if the store fails.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Only entries at or after this timestamp (RFC 3339).

parameters.name: until
parameters.description: Only entries at or before this timestamp (RFC 3339).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of entries to return (default 100).

post.summary:
POST /api/v1/build
Start a new build from multipart upload (Dockerfile + context tarball)
post.description:
Accepts a multipart form with:
- `dockerfile`: The Dockerfile content (optional if using runtime)
- `context`: A tarball containing the build context
- `config`: JSON configuration (`BuildRequest`)

# Errors

Returns an error if the request is invalid, context extraction fails, or the build
cannot be started.

post.summary:
POST /api/v1/build/json
Start a new build from JSON request with a context path on the server.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the context path is invalid or the build cannot be started.

get.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the build is not found.

# Errors

Returns an error if the build is not found or logs cannot be read.

get.summary:
GET /api/v1/build/{id}/stream
Stream build progress via Server-Sent Events.
get.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the build is not found or not active.

# Errors

Returns an error if the user is not authenticated.

post.summary: Force this node to become the cluster leader (disaster recovery).
post.description:
`POST /api/v1/cluster/force-leader`

DESTRUCTIVE operation for when the original leader is permanently lost.
Saves cluster state, shuts down Raft, writes a recovery marker.
The daemon must be restarted to complete recovery.

# Errors

Returns an error if the confirmation string is wrong, the Raft coordinator
is unavailable, this node is already the leader, or the recovery state
cannot be saved.

400.description: Invalid confirmation or leader still reachable

post.summary: Handle node heartbeat.
post.description:
`POST /api/v1/cluster/heartbeat`

Accepts resource usage data from worker nodes and proposes an
`UpdateNodeHeartbeat` to the Raft state machine.

# Panics

Panics if the system clock is before the Unix epoch.

post.summary: Handle a cluster join request.
post.description:
`POST /api/v1/cluster/join`

Validates the join token, assigns a Raft node ID, calls `raft.add_member()`,
and returns the assignment + peer list.

# Errors

Returns an error if the Raft coordinator is unavailable, the join token is
invalid, IP allocation fails, or the Raft membership change fails.

500.description: Failed to add member to Raft

get.summary: List all nodes visible in the Raft cluster state.
get.description:
`GET /api/v1/cluster/nodes`

# Errors

Returns an error if the cluster state cannot be read.

Returns all containers managed through this API. Optionally filter by
label using the `label` query parameter in `key=value` format.

# Errors

Returns an error if the user is not authenticated.

post.summary: Create and start a container.
post.description:
Pulls the image if needed, creates the container, and starts it.
Returns the container info including its assigned ID.

# Errors

Returns an error if image pull fails, container creation fails, or the
user lacks the operator role.

get.summary: Get details for a specific container.
get.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the container is not found or the user is not authenticated.

delete.description:
Sends a stop signal (with a 30-second timeout), then removes the container.

# Errors

Returns an error if the container is not found, stop/remove fails, or the
user lacks the operator role.

post.summary: Execute a command in a running container.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the container is not found, the command is invalid,
execution fails, or the user lacks the operator role.

post.summary: Send a signal to a running container.
post.description:
Mirrors Docker-compat `POST /containers/{id}/kill`. When the request body's
`signal` field is omitted, the runtime sends `SIGKILL`. Accepted signals
are `SIGKILL`, `SIGTERM`, `SIGINT`, `SIGHUP`, `SIGUSR1`, `SIGUSR2` (with
or without the `SIG` prefix); any other value is rejected with `400`.

# Errors

Returns `400` for unknown signals, `404` if the container is not found,
`403` if the caller lacks the `operator` role, `501` if the runtime does
not support `kill_container`, and `500` for other runtime errors.

get.description:
Returns the last N lines of container logs as plain text, or streams
logs as Server-Sent Events when `follow=true`.

# Errors

Returns an error if the container is not found or log retrieval fails.

parameters.name: tail
parameters.description: Number of tail lines to return

200.description: Container logs (plain text or SSE stream)

post.summary: Restart a container: stop then start.
post.description:
Composes `stop_container(timeout)` followed by `start_container`. Errors
during the stop phase are ignored (the container may already be stopped);
errors during the start phase are surfaced.

# Errors

Returns an error if the container is not found, the start phase fails, or
the user lacks the operator role.

post.summary: Start a previously-created container.
post.description:
Useful for re-starting a container that was stopped via
`POST /api/v1/containers/{id}/stop` without being removed.

# Errors

Returns an error if the container is not found, start fails, or the user
lacks the operator role.

get.description:
Returns CPU and memory usage statistics for the specified container.

# Errors

Returns an error if the container is not found or stats retrieval fails.

post.summary: Stop a running container.
post.description:
Sends the runtime's graceful stop signal and waits up to `timeout` seconds
before force-killing. The container is **not** removed; use
`DELETE /api/v1/containers/{id}` to stop-and-remove. Idempotent: calling
stop on an already-stopped container is not an error.

# Errors

Returns an error if the container is not found, stop fails, or the user
lacks the operator role.

get.summary: Wait for a container to exit and return its exit code.
get.description:
This endpoint blocks until the container exits. Useful for CI runners
that need to wait for a build/test container to complete.

# Errors

Returns an error if the container is not found or the wait fails.

get.description:
Returns a list of all registered cron jobs with their schedule information.

# Errors

Returns an error if the user is not authenticated.

Returns detailed information about a specific cron job.

# Errors

Returns an error if the cron job is not found.

put.description:
Disables a cron job, preventing it from running on schedule.
The job can still be manually triggered.

# Errors

Returns an error if the cron job is not found or the user lacks permission.

Enables a disabled cron job, allowing it to run on schedule.

# Errors

Returns an error if the cron job is not found or the user lacks permission.

post.summary: POST /api/v1/cron/{name}/trigger - Manually trigger a cron job
post.description:
Triggers an immediate execution of the cron job, regardless of its schedule.

# Errors

Returns an error if the cron job is not found or triggering fails.

get.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if storage access fails.

post.summary: Create a new deployment.
post.description:
When the daemon has orchestration wired (service manager, proxy, overlay),
this handler:
 1. Parses and validates the spec YAML
 2. Stores the deployment with status `Deploying`
 3. Spawns an async task that registers services, sets up overlays,
    configures the proxy, and scales services
 4. Returns immediately with `Deploying` status
 5. The async task updates the stored status to `Running` or `Failed`

Without orchestration wired, it stores the spec with `Pending` status.

# Errors

Returns an error if the spec is invalid or storage fails.

get.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the deployment is not found or storage access fails.

# Errors

Returns an error if the deployment or service is not found.

Returns service logs as plain text when `follow=false`, or as a Server-Sent
Events stream when `follow=true`.  In follow mode the server first emits the
last N lines (controlled by the `lines` parameter) and then continuously
polls for new output, emitting each new line as a `data:` SSE event.

# Errors

Returns an error if the deployment or service is not found, or the service
manager is not available.

200.description: Service logs (plain text or SSE stream)

post.summary: Scale a service.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the deployment or service is not found, scaling fails,
or the user lacks permission.

get.summary: Get deployment details (with live per-service health when available).
get.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the deployment is not found or storage access fails.

delete.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the deployment is not found, storage access fails, or teardown
encounters critical failures.

get.description:
Filter rules (see [`ListEnvironmentsQuery`]):

- `?project={id}` → environments belonging to that project.
- `?project=*` → every environment across every project + globals,
  merged and sorted by name.
- no `project` query → only global environments (`project_id IS NULL`).

# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Internal`] if the environment store fails.

parameters.name: project
parameters.description: Project id to filter by; '*' lists all; omit for globals only

post.summary: Create a new environment. Admin only.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] when the caller is not an admin,
[`ApiError::BadRequest`] for an empty name,
[`ApiError::Conflict`] when the `(name, project_id)` is already used, or
[`ApiError::Internal`] when the environment store fails.

409.description: Name already used in the given project

get.summary: Fetch a single environment by id.
get.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] if no environment with the given id exists,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the store fails.

delete.description:
Non-cascading: the operator must purge any secrets attached to the
environment before deletion (typically via
`DELETE /api/v1/secrets?env={id}&all=true`). If the environment still
has secrets, this endpoint returns 409.

# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] for non-admins, [`ApiError::NotFound`]
when the environment does not exist, [`ApiError::Conflict`] when secrets
remain, or [`ApiError::Internal`] when a backing store fails.

patch.summary: Rename / re-describe an environment. Admin only.
patch.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] for non-admins, [`ApiError::NotFound`]
when the environment does not exist, [`ApiError::BadRequest`] for an
empty replacement name, [`ApiError::Conflict`] when the new name collides
with another environment in the same scope, or [`ApiError::Internal`]
when the store fails.

get.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Internal`] if the group store fails.

post.summary: Create a new group. Admin only.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] when the caller is not an admin,
[`ApiError::BadRequest`] when the name is empty, or
[`ApiError::Internal`] if the store fails.

get.summary: Fetch a single group by id.
get.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] when no such group exists, or
[`ApiError::Internal`] when the store fails.

delete.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] when the caller is not an admin,
[`ApiError::NotFound`] when the group does not exist, or
[`ApiError::Internal`] if the store fails.

patch.summary: Update a group's name and/or description. Admin only.
patch.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] when the caller is not an admin,
[`ApiError::NotFound`] when the group does not exist, or
[`ApiError::Internal`] if the store fails.

post.summary: Add a member to a group. Admin only.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] when the caller is not an admin,

delete.description:

get.summary: List all cached images known to the runtime.
get.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if authentication fails or the runtime cannot enumerate
its image cache (for example, when the backend does not implement
`list_images`).

501.description: Runtime does not support image listing

post.summary: Pull an OCI image into the runtime's local cache.
post.description:
This is a blocking pull: the handler returns only after the image is
resolved and stored locally (or the pull fails). When `pull_policy` is
omitted the default is `"always"`, matching Docker-compat semantics for
`POST /images/create`. On success, the response echoes the reference and
best-effort `digest`/`size_bytes` resolved via `list_images()`.

# Errors

Returns an error if authentication fails, the reference is empty, the
image cannot be pulled, or the user lacks the operator role.

post.summary: Create a new tag pointing at an existing image.
post.description:
Docker-compat `POST /api/v1/images/tag`: takes `{ source, target }` and
asks the runtime to make `target` resolve to the same content as `source`.
Both references must be non-empty; `target` is split on the last `:` into
repository + tag (defaulting tag to `latest`).

# Errors

Returns `400` if the request body is malformed, `404` if the source image
is not in the cache, `403` if the caller lacks the `operator` role, `501`
if the runtime does not support tagging, and `500` for other runtime
errors.

delete.summary: Remove an image from the runtime's cache.
delete.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if authentication fails, the image cannot be found, or
the runtime backend does not support image removal.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Force removal even if the image is referenced by containers.

501.description: Runtime does not support image removal

get.summary: Get the current replica count for a service.
get.description:
This endpoint allows the scheduler to query the current state of a service.

# Errors

Returns an error if the service is not found or authentication is invalid.

401.description: Unauthorized - invalid or missing internal token

post.summary: Scale a service via internal scheduler request.
post.description:
This endpoint is called by the distributed scheduler leader to trigger
scaling operations on agent nodes. It uses a shared secret for authentication.

# Errors

Returns an error if the service is not found, scaling fails, or authentication
is invalid.

401.description: Unauthorized - invalid or missing internal token

post.summary: POST /`api/v1/jobs/{execution_id}/cancel` - Cancel a running execution
post.description:
Attempts to cancel a running or pending job execution.

# Errors

Returns an error if the execution is not found, already completed, or
cancellation fails.

get.description:
Returns the current status of a job execution, including logs if available.

# Errors

Returns an error if the execution is not found.

get.summary: GET /api/v1/jobs/{name}/executions - List executions for a job
get.description:
Returns a list of recent executions for the specified job.

# Errors

Returns an error if authentication fails.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of executions to return

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by status (pending, running, completed, failed)

post.summary: POST /api/v1/jobs/{name}/trigger - Trigger a job execution
post.description:
Starts a new execution of the specified job. Returns immediately with an
execution ID that can be used to track the job's progress.

# Errors

Returns an error if the job is not found or triggering fails.

get.description:
Returns a summary of every network defined in the system.

# Errors

Returns an error if the user is not authenticated.

post.summary: Create a new network.
post.description:
The network name must be unique. Returns the created spec.

# Errors

Returns an error if validation fails, the network already exists, or the
user lacks the operator role.

get.summary: Get a specific network by name.
get.description:
Returns the full `NetworkPolicySpec` for the named network.

# Errors

Returns an error if the network is not found or the user is not authenticated.

put.description:
Replaces the network definition for the given name. The name in the URL
must match the name in the body.

# Errors

Returns an error if the network is not found, the URL name does not match
the body, or the user lacks the operator role.

delete.description:
Permanently removes a network definition.

# Errors

Returns an error if the network is not found or the user lacks the operator role.

get.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if authentication fails.

post.summary: Generate a join token for new nodes to join the cluster.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if cluster management is not available or the user lacks permission.

get.summary: Get detailed information about a specific node.
get.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the node is not found.

post.summary: Update labels on a node.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the node is not found or the user lacks permission.

get.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Internal`] if the notifier store fails.

post.summary: Create a new notifier. Admin only.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] when the caller is not an admin,
[`ApiError::BadRequest`] for an empty name or kind/config mismatch,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the notifier store fails.

get.summary: Fetch a single notifier by id.
get.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] if no notifier with the given id exists,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the store fails.

delete.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] for non-admins, [`ApiError::NotFound`]
when the notifier does not exist, or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the
store fails.

patch.description:
Supports partial updates: only fields present in the request body are
changed.

# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] for non-admins, [`ApiError::NotFound`]
when the notifier does not exist, [`ApiError::BadRequest`] for
validation errors, or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the store fails.

post.summary: Send a test notification through a notifier. Admin only.
post.description:
For Slack and Discord, sends a test message via the configured webhook
URL. For generic webhooks, sends a JSON test payload. For SMTP, sends a
real email via [`lettre`] using the notifier's configured credentials.

# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] for non-admins, [`ApiError::NotFound`]
when the notifier does not exist, or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the
notification fails with a truly internal error. Upstream failures
(bad credentials, unreachable SMTP server, invalid addresses) are
returned with HTTP 200 and `success: false` in the body, matching the
convention established by the Slack/Discord/Webhook arms.

get.description:
Returns DNS server configuration and registered service count.
When DNS is not configured, returns a disabled status (not an error).

Returns the overlay network's IP allocation statistics including CIDR,
total IPs, allocated count, and utilization. Available on any node that
has an active overlay (not just leaders).

# Errors

Returns an error if the overlay is not initialized.

503.description: Not a leader node or overlay not initialized

get.description:
Returns peer information from the overlay transport's UAPI state.
When the overlay is not initialized, returns 503.

# Errors

Returns an error if the overlay network is not initialized.

Returns the current overlay network status including interface name,
node IP, CIDR, peer counts, and whether the transport is active.
Returns 503 if the overlay network is not initialized (e.g. host
networking mode).

# Errors

Returns an error if the overlay network is not initialized.

get.summary: List permissions for a subject (user or group).
get.description:
Exactly one of `user` or `group` must be provided.

# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::BadRequest`] when neither or both query parameters are

400.description: Must provide exactly one of user or group

post.summary: Grant a permission. Admin only.

get.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] if no project with the given id exists,

delete.summary: Delete a project. Admin only. Cascade-removes deployment links.
delete.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] for non-admins, [`ApiError::NotFound`]
when the project does not exist, or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the
store fails.

patch.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] for non-admins, [`ApiError::NotFound`]
when the project does not exist, [`ApiError::BadRequest`] for an empty
replacement name, [`ApiError::Conflict`] when the new name collides, or

get.summary: List deployment names linked to a project.
get.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] when the project does not exist,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the store fails.

post.summary: Link a deployment to a project.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] when the project does not exist,
[`ApiError::BadRequest`] for an empty deployment name, or
[`ApiError::Internal`] when the store fails.

delete.summary: Unlink a deployment from a project.
delete.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] when the project or link does not exist,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the store fails.

post.summary:
Clone the project's git repository (or fast-forward pull if the working
copy already exists) into `{clone_root}/{project_id}` and return the
resulting HEAD SHA.
post.description:
Authentication is resolved from `git_credential_id`: when set, the
matching [`GitCredential`](zlayer_secrets::GitCredential) is looked up in
the [`GitCredentialStore`](zlayer_secrets::GitCredentialStore) and its
kind determines whether we build a PAT or SSH auth config. When the
project has no credential id, or the state has no git credential store
attached, we fall back to anonymous auth.

Admin only.

# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] for non-admins, [`ApiError::NotFound`]
when the project does not exist, [`ApiError::BadRequest`] when the
project has no `git_url` or its `git_credential_id` cannot be resolved,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] on any underlying git / credential store
failure.

400.description: Project has no git URL configured

get.summary: Get webhook configuration for a project.
get.description:
Returns the webhook URL pattern and the HMAC secret. Generates the
secret on first call if it does not exist.

Auth required (any authenticated user).

# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] when the project does not exist,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] on store failures.

post.summary: Rotate (regenerate) the webhook secret for a project.
post.description:
Admin only.

# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] for non-admins,
[`ApiError::NotFound`] when the project does not exist,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] on store failures.

get.description:
Returns each service's backend group with its strategy, health status, and
active connection counts.

# Errors

Returns `ApiError::ServiceUnavailable` if the load balancer is not initialised.

Returns the full list of L7 routes with their host patterns, path prefixes,
backends, and protocol information.

# Errors

Returns `ApiError::ServiceUnavailable` if the service registry is not initialised.

Returns all TCP and UDP stream proxies with their listen ports, service
names, and backends.

# Errors

Returns `ApiError::ServiceUnavailable` if the stream registry is not initialised.

Returns cached certificate domains with metadata (expiry, fingerprint)
when available.

# Errors

Returns `ApiError::ServiceUnavailable` if the certificate manager is not initialised.

Scope resolution: see [`resolve_scope`].

# Errors

Returns an error if storage access fails.

post.summary: Create or update a secret.
post.description:
Stores a new secret or updates an existing one. The secret value is
encrypted at rest and the version number is incremented on updates.

Scope resolution: see [`resolve_scope`].

# Errors

Returns an error if validation fails, storage operations fail, or the
caller lacks permission.

post.summary: Bulk-import secrets from a dotenv-style payload (`KEY=value\n…`).
post.description:
Each non-empty, non-comment line is parsed into a (name, value) pair and
written to the env's scope. Lines that fail to parse are returned in
`errors` and do not abort the import. Each successful write is counted
as either `created` or `updated`.

# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] for non-admins, [`ApiError::NotFound`]
when the environment id is unknown, or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the
secrets store fails.

get.summary:
Get metadata for a specific secret. With `?reveal=true` (admin only),
the response also includes the plaintext `value`.
get.description:
Scope resolution: see [`resolve_scope_get`].

# Errors

Returns an error if the secret is not found, the caller is unauthorised
to reveal, or storage access fails.

delete.description:
Scope resolution: see [`resolve_scope_get`] (the same query type is
reused minus `reveal`).

# Errors

Returns an error if the secret is not found, storage access fails, or
the caller lacks permission.

get.description:
Returns the current state of SQLite-to-S3 replication including whether
it is enabled, the last sync time, and pending change count.

# Errors

Returns an error if authentication fails.

# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Internal`] if the sync store fails.

post.summary: Create a new sync.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Validation`] if required fields are missing, or
[`ApiError::Conflict`] if a sync with the same name already exists.

delete.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] if the sync id does not exist, or
[`ApiError::Internal`] if the store operation fails.

post.summary: Apply a sync — real reconcile against the API.
post.description:
For each resource in the manifest directory:
- `deployment` YAMLs are parsed, validated, and upserted into the
  deployment store.
- `job` / `cron` kinds are recorded as skipped with an explanatory message
  (standalone job/cron storage does not yet exist; schedule services via
  `DeploymentSpec` instead).
- Unknown kinds are skipped with `"unknown kind: {kind}"`.

If `sync.delete_missing` is `true`, deployments present in the store but
absent from the manifest directory are deleted. Otherwise deletions are
recorded as skipped with reason `"delete_missing=false"`.

On success the sync's `last_applied_sha` is refreshed from the current
checkout and persisted.

# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] if the sync id does not exist, or
[`ApiError::Internal`] if scanning fails.

get.summary: Compute a diff for a sync (scan git path vs. remote resources).
get.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] if the sync id does not exist, or
[`ApiError::Internal`] if scanning fails.

post.summary: Prune dangling / unused images from the runtime's cache.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if authentication fails or the runtime backend does not
support pruning.

get.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Internal`] if the task store fails.

parameters.name: project_id
parameters.description: Filter by project id; omit to list all tasks

post.summary: Create a new task. Admin only.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] when the caller is not an admin,
[`ApiError::BadRequest`] for an empty name or body,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the task store fails.

get.summary: Fetch a single task by id.
get.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] if no task with the given id exists,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the store fails.

delete.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] for non-admins, [`ApiError::NotFound`]
when the task does not exist, or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the
store fails.

post.summary: Execute a task synchronously. Admin only.
post.description:
Runs the task's body via `sh -c` on Unix, captures stdout/stderr,
and returns the completed `TaskRun`.

# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] for non-admins, [`ApiError::NotFound`]
when the task does not exist, [`ApiError::NotImplemented`] on non-Unix
platforms, or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the store or execution fails.

get.summary: List past runs for a task, most recent first.
get.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] if the task does not exist,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the store fails.

# Errors

Returns an error if authentication fails.

post.summary: Create a new tunnel token.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if validation fails or authentication is invalid.

post.summary: Create a node-to-node tunnel.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if validation fails or the user lacks permission.

delete.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the tunnel is not found or the user lacks permission.

# Errors

Returns an error if the tunnel is not found.

get.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the tunnel is not found.

# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] when the caller is not an admin, or
[`ApiError::Internal`] if the user store fails.

post.summary: Create a new user. Admin only.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] when the caller is not an admin,
[`ApiError::BadRequest`] when the body is incomplete,
[`ApiError::Conflict`] when the email is already registered, or
[`ApiError::Internal`] if a backing store fails.

get.summary:
Fetch a single user. Admins can read any record; regular users can read
only their own.
get.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] when a non-admin tries to read a
different user, [`ApiError::NotFound`] when no such user exists, or
[`ApiError::Internal`] when the store fails.

delete.summary: Delete a user. Admin only. Callers cannot delete their own account.
delete.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] when the caller is not an admin,
[`ApiError::BadRequest`] when an admin tries to delete themselves,
[`ApiError::NotFound`] when the user does not exist, or
[`ApiError::Internal`] if a backing store fails.

patch.summary: Update a user's mutable fields. Admin only.
patch.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] when the caller is not an admin,
[`ApiError::NotFound`] when the user does not exist, or
[`ApiError::Internal`] if the user store fails.

post.summary:
Set a user's password. Admins may change any user's password; regular
users may only change their own, and must supply `current_password`.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::BadRequest`] for missing fields,
[`ApiError::Unauthorized`] for an incorrect current password,
[`ApiError::Forbidden`] when the caller is not allowed to change this
user's password, [`ApiError::NotFound`] when the user does not exist,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] if a backing store fails.

get.description:
Filter rules (see [`ListVariablesQuery`]):

- `?scope={id}` -> variables belonging to that scope.
- no `scope` query -> only global variables (`scope IS NULL`).

# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Internal`] if the variable store fails.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Scope (project id) to filter by; omit for globals only

post.summary: Create a new variable. Admin only.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] when the caller is not an admin,
[`ApiError::BadRequest`] for an empty name,
[`ApiError::Conflict`] when the `(name, scope)` is already used, or
[`ApiError::Internal`] when the variable store fails.

409.description: Name already used in the given scope

get.summary: Fetch a single variable by id.
get.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] if no variable with the given id exists,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the store fails.

delete.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] for non-admins, [`ApiError::NotFound`]
when the variable does not exist, or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the
store fails.

patch.summary: Update a variable's name and/or value. Admin only.
patch.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] for non-admins, [`ApiError::NotFound`]
when the variable does not exist, [`ApiError::BadRequest`] for an
empty replacement name, [`ApiError::Conflict`] when the new name collides
with another variable in the same scope, or [`ApiError::Internal`]
when the store fails.

get.description:
Enumerates subdirectories under the volume base directory and returns
metadata for each one.

# Errors

Returns an error if the volume directory cannot be read.

delete.description:
Removes a volume directory from disk. By default, only empty volumes
can be deleted. Use `?force=true` to remove non-empty volumes.

# Errors

Returns an error if the volume is not found or cannot be removed.

get.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Internal`] if the workflow store fails.

post.summary: Create a new workflow. Admin only.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] when the caller is not an admin,
[`ApiError::BadRequest`] for an empty name or empty steps list,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the workflow store fails.

get.summary: Fetch a single workflow by id.
get.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] if no workflow with the given id exists,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the store fails.

delete.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] for non-admins, [`ApiError::NotFound`]
when the workflow does not exist, or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the
store fails.

post.summary: Execute a workflow synchronously. Admin only.
post.description:
Iterates steps sequentially:
- `RunTask` — looks up the task and executes its `body` via `sh -c`.
- `BuildProject` — clones / fast-forwards the project's git repo, then
  registers and awaits a real buildah build.
- `DeployProject` — reads the project's `deploy_spec_path` from its
  checked-out working copy, parses it as a `DeploymentSpec`, and upserts
  it into the deployment store.
- `ApplySync` — delegates to [`apply_sync_inner`] for the real reconcile.

If a step fails and has an `on_failure` handler, that handler runs before
the workflow aborts. Subsequent steps are marked `"skipped"`.

# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::Forbidden`] for non-admins, [`ApiError::NotFound`]
when the workflow does not exist, or [`ApiError::Internal`] when
execution or recording fails.

get.summary: List past runs for a workflow, most recent first.
get.description:
# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] if the workflow does not exist,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] when the store fails.

post.summary: Bootstrap the very first admin user. Returns 409 if any user exists.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the user store or credential store is not configured,
if any user already exists, if the request is missing required fields, or
if the underlying stores fail.

get.summary: Rotate the CSRF double-submit token for the current session.

post.summary: Sign in an existing user.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if credentials are invalid, the user is disabled, or any
backing store fails.

post.summary: Clear the session + CSRF cookies.

get.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the user store is not configured, if the user no longer
exists, or if the store fails.

post.summary: Receive a webhook push event and trigger a project pull.
post.description:
This endpoint is **unauthenticated** -- it relies on HMAC signature
verification against the per-project webhook secret.

Supported providers: `github`, `gitea`, `forgejo`, `gitlab`.

# Errors

Returns [`ApiError::NotFound`] when no webhook secret is configured,
[`ApiError::Forbidden`] when signature verification fails,
[`ApiError::BadRequest`] for unsupported providers or missing git URL,
or [`ApiError::Internal`] on git operation failures.

parameters.name: provider
parameters.description: Git host provider (github, gitea, forgejo, gitlab)

requestBody.description: Raw webhook payload from the git host

400.description: Bad request (unsupported provider or project has no git URL)

404.description: Project not found or no webhook secret configured

AuditEntry.description:
A recorded audit log entry capturing who did what, when, and to which
resource.

id.description: UUID identifier of this audit entry.

resource_id.description: The specific resource id, if applicable.

resource_kind.description:
The kind of resource acted upon (e.g. `"deployment"`, `"user"`,
`"project"`).

user_id.description: The user who performed the action.

BackendGroupInfo.description: A load-balancer backend group for one service.

BackendInfo.description: Information about a single backend in a load-balancer group.

healthy.description: Whether the backend is currently healthy.

runtime.description: Use runtime template instead of Dockerfile

tags.description: Tags to apply to the image

BuildRequestWithContext.description: Build request with server-side context path

context_path.description: Path to the build context on the server

runtime.description: Use runtime template instead of Dockerfile

errors.description: Per-line errors. Empty when every line parsed and stored cleanly.

CertInfo.description: Information about a loaded TLS certificate.

fingerprint.description: SHA-256 fingerprint, if metadata is available.

needs_renewal.description: Whether the certificate needs renewal (within 30 days of expiry).

not_after.description: Certificate expiry (ISO-8601), if metadata is available.

not_before.description: Certificate validity start (ISO-8601), if metadata is available.

cpu_total.description: Total CPU cores on the joining node

overlay_ip.description: Assigned overlay IP for the new node

role.description: Role assigned to this node: "voter" or "learner"

ClusterNodeSummary.description: Summary of a cluster node for listing.

cpu_total.description: Total CPU cores on this node

is_leader.description: Whether this node is the Raft leader

overlay_ip.description: Overlay network IP assigned to this node

registered_at.description: When the node was registered (Unix timestamp ms)

role.description: Role in the Raft cluster: "leader", "voter", or "learner"

ClusterPeer.description: Summary of an existing cluster peer returned in join response.

ContainerExecRequest.description: Exec request for running a command in a container

ContainerInfo.description: Container information returned by the API

memory_limit.description: Memory limit in bytes (`u64::MAX` if unlimited)

memory_percent.description: Memory usage as percentage of limit

ContainerWaitResponse.description: Wait response with container exit code

CreateContainerRequest.description: Request to create and start a container

description.description: Free-form description shown in the UI.

name.description: Display name. Must be unique within the chosen `project_id` namespace.

value.description: PAT or SSH key content (stored encrypted, never returned).

CreateNodeTunnelRequest.description: Request to create a node-to-node tunnel

local_port.description: Local port on the source node

remote_port.description: Remote port on the destination node

CreateNodeTunnelResponse.description: Response after creating a node-to-node tunnel

auto_deploy.description: Enable automatic deploy on new commits.

build_path.description: Relative build path within the repo.

deploy_spec_path.description:
Relative path (inside the cloned repo) to a `DeploymentSpec` YAML
that the workflow `DeployProject` action should apply.

poll_interval_secs.description: Polling interval in seconds (None = no polling).

password.description: Password or token value (stored encrypted, never returned).

CreateSecretRequest.description:
Request to create or update a secret.

`scope` is optional and only honored on the legacy code path — when set
alongside `?environment=`, the request is rejected.

scope.description:
Optional explicit scope (legacy form). Mutually exclusive with the
`?environment=` query parameter.

value.description: The secret value (will be encrypted at rest).

auto_apply.description: Whether the sync should automatically apply on pull.

delete_missing.description:
Whether `apply` should delete resources on the API that are missing
from the manifest directory. Defaults to `false` (the safer choice).

git_path.description: Path within the project's checkout to scan for resource YAMLs.

CreateTunnelRequest.description: Request to create a new tunnel token

services.description: Optional list of services this tunnel is allowed to expose

CreateTunnelResponse.description: Response after creating a tunnel token

created_at.description: When the tunnel was created (Unix timestamp)

token.description: The tunnel token to use for authentication

name.description:
Variable name (e.g. `"APP_VERSION"`). Must be unique within the chosen
scope.

last_run.description: When the job last ran (ISO 8601 format)

DeploymentDetails.description: Deployment details (enhanced with per-service health and endpoints)

confirm.description: Confirmation string -- must be `"CONFIRM_FORCE_LEADER"` to prevent accidents

preserved_nodes.description: Number of cluster nodes whose state was preserved

preserved_services.description: Number of services whose state was preserved

GitCredentialResponse.description: Git credential metadata (returned by list/create; no secret value).

GpuInfoSummary.description: Summary of a GPU on a node, stored in Raft cluster state

GpuUtilizationReport.description: Per-GPU utilization snapshot reported in node heartbeats

memory_used_mb.description: GPU memory currently used in MB

resource_id.description: A specific resource id, or omit for a wildcard (all resources of that kind).

subject_kind.description: Whether the subject is a user or a group.

members.description: List of user ids in the group.

HeartbeatRequest.description: Heartbeat request from a worker node.

ImageInfoDto.description:
Serializable wrapper for [`ImageInfo`] so we can attach `ToSchema` here
(the underlying type in `zlayer-agent` can't depend on `utoipa`).

allocated_ips.description: List of allocated IP addresses (only included if requested)

total_ips.description: Total available IPs in the range

status.description: Current status (pending, initializing, running, completed, failed, cancelled)

JoinTokenResponse.description: Join token response for cluster joining

allocated_ip.description: Allocated IP address for the joining node

leader_public_key.description: Leader's public key for secure communication

token.description: Join token for authenticating the new node

KillContainerRequest.description:
Request body for killing (sending a signal to) a container. Matches the
Docker-compat `POST /containers/{id}/kill` shape.

signal.description:
Signal name to send (e.g. `"SIGTERM"`, `"SIGINT"`). Accepts both the
`SIG`-prefixed and bare forms. When omitted, defaults to `SIGKILL`.

deployment_name.description: Name of the deployment to link.

registered_at.description: When the node was registered (Unix timestamp)

headers.description: Extra headers to send with the request.

is_leader.description: Whether this node is the cluster leader

last_check.description: Last health check timestamp (unix epoch seconds)

failure_count.description: Number of consecutive health check failures

last_check.description: Last health check timestamp (unix epoch seconds)

PermissionLevel.description:
Access level for a resource permission, ordered from least to most
privilege.

branch.description: Branch checked out in the working copy.

git_url.description: Git URL that was cloned / fetched.

path.description: Absolute path to the working copy on disk.

project_id.description: Project id the pull was performed for.

sha.description: HEAD commit SHA after the pull.

deleted.description: Image references or digests that were removed.

PullImageRequest.description: Request body for [`pull_image_handler`]. Blocking pull of an OCI image.

pull_policy.description:
Pull policy override. Accepts `"always"`, `"if_not_present"`, or
`"never"`. Defaults to `"always"` when omitted.

reference.description: OCI image reference to pull, e.g. `docker.io/library/nginx:latest`.

PullImageResponse.description:
Response body for [`pull_image_handler`]. Reports the pulled reference
and, when the backend exposes it via `list_images`, the resolved digest
and on-disk size.

digest.description: Content-addressed digest (`sha256:...`) if the runtime reports one.

size_bytes.description: On-disk size in bytes if the runtime reports one.

RegisteredServiceInfo.description: Information about a registered service in a tunnel

remote_port.description: Remote port exposed on the server

RegistryAuthTypeSchema.description: Authentication method for a registry credential (`OpenAPI` schema).

RegistryCredentialResponse.description: Registry credential metadata (returned by list/create; no password).

ReplicationInfo.description: Replication detail within the storage status response

last_sync.description: ISO-8601 timestamp of the last successful sync, or null

pending_changes.description: Number of WAL segments pending upload

RestartContainerRequest.description:
Request body for restarting a container. Matches the Docker-compat
`POST /containers/{id}/restart` shape.

timeout.description:
Graceful shutdown timeout in seconds before the runtime force-kills
the container. Defaults to 30 seconds when omitted.

RouteInfo.description: Information about a single registered route.

backends.description: Backend addresses currently assigned to this route.

host.description: Host pattern (e.g. `*.example.com`), or `null` for any host.

path_prefix.description: Path prefix matched by this route.

strip_prefix.description: Whether the matched prefix is stripped before forwarding.

SecretMetadataResponse.description:
Response containing secret metadata. Never includes the value unless
the caller is on the explicit `?reveal=true` admin path, in which case
`value` is populated.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp when the secret was created.

updated_at.description: Unix timestamp when the secret was last updated.

value.description: Plaintext value — populated only on `?reveal=true` admin reads.

version.description: Version number of the secret (incremented on each update).

ServiceHealthInfo.description: Per-service health info included in deployment details

current_password.description:
Required when the caller is setting their own password (non-admin
path). Admins changing someone else's password may omit this.

StepResult.description: Result of executing a single step in a workflow run.

StopContainerRequest.description:
Request body for stopping a container. Matches the Docker-compat
`POST /containers/{id}/stop` shape.

timeout.description:
Graceful shutdown timeout in seconds before the runtime force-kills
the container. Defaults to 30 seconds when omitted.

StoredEnvironment.description:
A deployment/runtime environment (e.g. "dev", "staging", "prod").

Each environment is an isolated namespace for secrets and, later,
deployments. Optionally belongs to a `Project` (added in Phase 5) — when
`project_id` is `None`, the environment is global.

description.description: Free-form description shown in the UI.

name.description: Display name (e.g. "dev"). Unique within a given `project_id`.

project_id.description: Project id this environment belongs to. `None` = global.

updated_at.description: When the environment was last updated.

StoredNotifier.description:
A stored notifier — a named notification channel that fires alerts to
Slack, Discord, a generic webhook, or SMTP when triggered.

config.description: Channel-specific configuration (webhook URL, SMTP settings, etc.).

enabled.description: Whether this notifier is active. Disabled notifiers are skipped.

updated_at.description: When the notifier was last updated.

StoredPermission.description:
A stored permission grant binding a subject (user or group) to a resource
with a specific access level.

id.description: UUID identifier of this permission grant.

resource_id.description:
A specific resource id, or `None` for a wildcard (all resources of
that kind).

subject_kind.description: Whether the subject is a user or a group.

StoredProject.description:
A project bundles a git source, build configuration, registry credential
reference, linked deployments, and a default environment.

auto_deploy.description:
Whether new commits on the tracked branch should automatically
trigger a build + deploy cycle.

default_environment_id.description: Reference to the default environment for this project.

deploy_spec_path.description:
Relative path (inside the cloned repo) to a `DeploymentSpec` YAML that
the workflow `DeployProject` action should apply.

When `None`, the workflow `DeployProject` action fails with a clear
"no deploy spec configured" error rather than silently succeeding —
callers are expected to set this explicitly via `project edit`.

description.description: Free-form description shown in the UI.

poll_interval_secs.description:
If set, the daemon polls the remote for new commits every N seconds.
`None` disables polling (the project is only updated via manual pull
or webhook).

updated_at.description: When the project was last updated.

StoredSync.description:
A stored sync resource (persistent record of a git-backed resource set).

A sync points at a directory within a project's checkout that contains
`ZLayer` resource YAMLs. On diff/apply the directory is scanned, compared
against current API state, and reconciled.

auto_apply.description: Whether the sync should automatically apply on pull.

delete_missing.description:
Whether the sync apply should delete resources that are present on the
API but missing from the manifest directory. Defaults to `false` —
the safer behaviour, which skips deletes and only creates/updates.

git_path.description: Path within the project's checkout to scan for resource YAMLs.

last_applied_sha.description: The commit SHA at which this sync was last applied.

project_id.description: Linked project id (the git checkout to scan).

updated_at.description: When the sync was last updated.

StoredTask.description:
A stored task — a named runnable script that can be executed on demand.

Tasks can be global (`project_id = None`) or project-scoped
(`project_id = Some(project_id)`).

project_id.description: Project id this task belongs to. `None` = global.

updated_at.description: When the task was last updated.

StoredUserGroup.description: A stored user group for role-based access control.

updated_at.description: When the group was last updated.

StoredVariable.description:
A stored variable — a plaintext key-value pair for template substitution
in deployment specs. Variables are NOT encrypted (unlike secrets). They
live in their own storage, separate from the encrypted secrets store.

Variables can be global (`scope = None`) or project-scoped
(`scope = Some(project_id)`).

name.description:
Variable name (e.g. `"APP_VERSION"`, `"LOG_LEVEL"`). Unique within a
given scope.

scope.description: Scope: project id or `None` for global.

updated_at.description: When the variable was last updated.

StoredWorkflow.description:
A stored workflow — a named sequence of steps forming a DAG that
composes tasks, project builds, deploys, and sync applies.

steps.description: Ordered list of steps to execute sequentially.

updated_at.description: When the workflow was last updated.

StreamBackendInfo.description: Information about a single L4 stream proxy backend.

StreamInfo.description: Information about a single L4 stream proxy.

SubjectKind.description: Whether a permission subject is a user or a group.

SyncApplyResponse.description:
Response for a real apply. Reports per-resource outcomes, the current
commit SHA the sync was applied at (when known), and a short human-readable
summary for CLI display.

NOTE: This is a breaking change from the previous dry-run-only
`{ diff, message }` shape. Clients inspecting the apply response directly
must be updated — callers that only consumed the HTTP status stay working.

applied_sha.description: Commit SHA the sync was applied against (when resolvable).

summary.description:
Aggregate summary suitable for CLI output, e.g.
`"3 created, 2 updated, 1 deleted, 0 skipped"`.

SyncResourceResponse.description: A single resource in the diff output.

SyncResourceResult.description: Result of reconciling a single resource during apply.

error.description:
Optional error message (`status == "error"`) or skip reason
(`action == "skip"`). Omitted on successful `"ok"` results.

resource.description: The source manifest path (or remote resource name for deletions).

TagImageRequest.description:
Request body for [`tag_image_handler`]. Matches Docker-compat
`docker tag` semantics: create a new reference (`target`) pointing at an
already-cached image (`source`).

TaskRun.description: A recorded execution of a task.

exit_code.description: Process exit code (`None` if the task could not be started).

finished_at.description: When the run finished (`None` if it has not finished yet).

detect_files.description: Files that indicate this runtime should be used

success.description: Whether the test notification was sent successfully.

TriggerCronResponse.description: Response after triggering a cron job

created_at.description: When the tunnel was created (Unix timestamp)

last_connected.description: Last time the tunnel connected (Unix timestamp, if ever)

created_at.description: When the tunnel was created (Unix timestamp)

last_connected.description: Last time the tunnel connected (Unix timestamp, if ever)

UpdateEnvironmentRequest.description: Body for `PATCH /api/v1/environments/{id}`. All fields are optional.

description.description: New description. Pass `Some("")` to clear, omit to leave unchanged.

name.description: New display name. Will be re-checked for uniqueness.

UpdateProjectRequest.description: Body for `PATCH /api/v1/projects/{id}`. All fields are optional.

auto_deploy.description: Enable or disable automatic deploy on new commits.

deploy_spec_path.description:
New path (inside the cloned repo) to the `DeploymentSpec` YAML that
workflow `DeployProject` actions should apply. Pass `""` to clear.

poll_interval_secs.description: Set polling interval in seconds. `null` disables polling.

UpdateUserRequest.description:
Body for `PATCH /api/v1/users/{id}`. All fields are optional so a caller
can update a single attribute without touching the others.

UpdateVariableRequest.description: Body for `PATCH /api/v1/variables/{id}`. All fields are optional.

name.description: New variable name. Will be re-checked for uniqueness.

UserRole.description:
User role. Admins can do everything; regular users are constrained by
per-resource permissions (added in a later phase).

UserView.description: Response shape used by login + bootstrap + me.

VolumeSummary.description: Summary of a single volume returned by the list endpoint.

size_bytes.description: Approximate size in bytes (sum of files in the volume directory)

secret.description: The HMAC secret to paste into the git host.

url.description: Full URL to configure in the git host's webhook settings.

WebhookResponse.description: Response for the public webhook receiver.

sha.description: HEAD commit SHA after the pull.

oneOf.description: Apply a sync resource by id.

WorkflowAction.description: The action a workflow step performs.

WorkflowRun.description: A recorded execution of a workflow.

finished_at.description: When the run finished (`None` if still running).

WorkflowRunStatus.description: Overall status of a workflow run.

WorkflowStep.description: A single step in a workflow.

on_failure.description: Name of another step (or task id) to run on failure.

tags.name: Images
tags.description: OCI image management (list, pull, tag, remove, prune)

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Git push webhook receiver and configuration

tags.name: Tasks
tags.description: Named runnable script management and execution

tags.name: Groups
